subject: Computer Networking
title: Edge Computing and the Web
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Edge Computing and the Web.

hook.text: Why send a request to a data center 5,000 miles away? Edge computing runs code close to the user.

buildup.text: Edge nodes sit in ISP facilities and CDN PoPs. They run serverless functions milliseconds from the user.

discovery.text: Cloudflare Workers run in 300+ cities. Vercel Edge Functions deploy globally. Latency drops dramatically.

twist.text: Edge has limits: small memory, short execution times, and no persistent storage. It's not a full server.

climax.text: Edge handles auth, redirects, A/B tests, and personalization. Light logic, big speed gains.

punchline.text: Edge computing: less distance, less latency, faster web.

quiz.question: What is a limitation of edge computing?
options: Small memory, short execution times, and no persistent storage
It only works in the United States
